payout_recommendation.rationale: PRs beacon-skill#820 and agentfolio-mcp-server#5 submitted with 3/4 deliverables claimed at 175/200 RTC. Substantial integration work with real PRs and evidence of effort.

rankings.justification: Claim #7338 references two actual PRs with numbers (#820, #5) and specific deliverables; the partial completion claim (175/200 RTC for 3/4 tasks) is plausible and consistent with tiered payment bounty structure.

payout_recommendation.rationale: GitHub Action with real PR #7341 submitted, but wallet listed as 'zhaog100' is not an RTC-format address. Either a display name or invalid address. Verify before paying.

rankings.justification: Claim #7342 for GitHub Action RTC Award lists wallet as 'zhaog100' which does not match the RTC-prefix wallet address format. This may be a username instead of a payment address, requiring clarification from maintainers before payout.

payout_recommendation.rationale: 3 haiku submitted with hardware claims but haiku #2 (riscv) lacks hardware photo proof per bounty #2844 requirements ('5 RTC each, 10 RTC w/ proof'). Only haiku #1 and #3 have hardware specifications.

rankings.justification: Claim #7356 for 3 Hybrid Haikus claims vintage_powerpc (1.4 GHz Power Mac G4), riscv (Sipeed MaixIII @ 1GHz), and apple_silicon, but bounty #2844 specifies '10 RTC w/ proof' for photo verification and only two haiku name hardware specifics, not actual photo proof.

payout_recommendation.rationale: PR review of test_contributor_registry.py is technically substantive (SQL injection patterns, SECRET_KEY config, input validation gaps) but the review link is placeholder ('pullrequestreview-xxx'); no actual GitHub review link provided.

rankings.justification: Claim #7357 references PR #2695 with real technical observations (SQL injection in test code, missing Flask SECRET_KEY) but the review link uses a placeholder fragment rather than a real review ID.

payout_recommendation.rationale: PR review of SECURITY.md is minimal ('Approved the PR') without specific observations, and wallet 'AhqbFaPBPLMMiaLDzA9WhQcyvv4hMxiteLhPk3NhG1iG' is non-RTC format; possibly a test or alternate wallet, but cannot verify against bounty #6885 policy requiring one canonical RTC wallet.

rankings.justification: Claim #7348 from @jaxint lists wallet 'AhqbFaPBPLMMiaLDzA9WhQcyvv4hMxiteLhPk3NhG1iG' which appears to be an Ed25519 address (starts with 'A' not 'RTC'), violating bounty #6885's 'One canonical RTC wallet per contributor' policy. The review body is also minimal with no specific observations.
